title: Why Checking Your Portfolio Daily Costs You $47,000
hook: You check your investment app every morning thinking you're being responsible — that one daily habit is quietly costing you forty-seven thousand dollars.
angle: DALBAR's 30-year study shows average investors earn 2.9%/yr vs 7.5% for the S&P 500 — the entire gap is explained by panic-selling on red days driven by loss aversion, not market underperformance
audience: Retail investors, 401k holders, anyone who obsessively monitors their portfolio
key_numbers: losses feel 2x worse than gains (loss aversion)
+2% per year for monthly vs daily checkers
